title: Wedelia xylopoda
intro: Wedelia xylopoda, also known as Mexican Plantain, is a perennial herb belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to Central and South America. Traditionally, Mexican Plantain has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Wedelia xylopoda include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional medicine, Mexican Plantain is commonly prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture to alleviate symptoms, and is also used in topical applications to soothe skin conditions.
taxonomy: Wedelia xylopoda is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to Mexico and Central America. The plant is classified in the genus Wedelia, which comprises about 40 species of annuals and perennials. Wedelia xylopoda is further classified in the subgenus Wedelia and the tribe Heliantheae. It is a herbaceous plant with a sprawling habit and produces small yellow flowers.
uses: Wedelia xylopoda, a plant native to tropical regions, has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves are known to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, fever, and infections. Its leaves and flowers are also used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. In addition, the plant has been used to treat digestive problems, including diarrhea and dysentery. Further research is needed to confirm the full range of medicinal uses for Wedelia xylopoda.

These constituents have been reported to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
